mission.title: Install and Configure SurrealDB
mission.description: SurrealDB is a scalable, distributed, collaborative, document-graph database designed for the realtime web. It is suitable for applications requiring flexible data models and real-time capabilities. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and management compared to traditional databases.

steps.title: Add the SurrealDB Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official SurrealDB Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add surrealdb https://charts.surrealdb.com

helm repo update

```

> This is the official repository for SurrealDB Helm charts.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install SurrealDB with Helm
steps.description:
This step installs SurrealDB in the specified namespace using the Helm chart.

```bash

kubectl create namespace surrealdb

helm install surrealdb surrealdb/surrealdb --namespace surrealdb --version 3.0.2

```

> Creates a namespace for SurrealDB.
> Installs SurrealDB with the specified version.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource (CR) for SurrealDB to demonstrate its functionality.

```bash

cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: surrealdb.com/v1
kind: SurrealDB
metadata:
  name: example-surrealdb
  namespace: surrealdb
spec:
  replicas: 1
  storage:
    size: 1Gi
EOF

```

> Creates a Custom Resource for SurrealDB.
commands:
cat <<EOF | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: surrealdb.com/v1
kind: SurrealDB
metadata:
  name: example-surrealdb
  namespace: surrealdb
spec:
  replicas: 1
  storage:
    size: 1Gi
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Custom Resource is ready
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Custom Resource to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get surrealdb -n surrealdb

kubectl describe surrealdb example-surrealdb -n surrealdb

```

> Lists the SurrealDB resources in the namespace.
> Describes the specific SurrealDB resource to check its status.

uninstall.title: Uninstall SurrealDB
uninstall.description:
This step removes the SurrealDB installation and all associated resources. Be aware that this will result in data loss.

```bash

helm uninstall surrealdb --namespace surrealdb

kubectl delete namespace surrealdb

```

> Uninstalls the SurrealDB release.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade SurrealDB
upgrade.description:
This step includes backing up data, upgrading the installation, and verifying the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get surrealdb example-surrealdb -n surrealdb -o yaml > backup-surrealdb.yaml

helm upgrade surrealdb surrealdb/surrealdb --namespace surrealdb --version 3.0.2

kubectl get surrealdb -n surrealdb

```

> Backup the current configuration.
> Upgrades SurrealDB to the specified version.
> Verifies that the SurrealDB resource is running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get surrealdb example-surrealdb -n surrealdb -o yaml > backup-surrealdb.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to database'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The SurrealDB pod may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the pod status with 'kubectl get pods -n surrealdb' and logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n surrealdb'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient storage available'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The persistent volume claim may not have enough storage allocated.

**Fix:**
Modify the storage size in the Custom Resource and reapply it.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Custom Resource not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Custom Resource may not have been created successfully.

**Fix:**
Recreate the Custom Resource using the provided YAML configuration.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Helm release not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Helm release may have been deleted or not installed.

**Fix:**
Reinstall SurrealDB using the Helm install command.

versionNotes.changes: Introduced new features for improved scalability and performance, including enhanced query capabilities.
versionNotes.deprecations: No deprecations noted in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Ensure that any existing configurations are compatible with the new version.

resolution.summary: You should see the SurrealDB resource listed with 'kubectl get surrealdb -n surrealdb' and its status should be 'Running'.

    size: 1Gi
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary resources.
